revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 長濱神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|24.2|N|138|52|30.8|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜69 |位置 = {{coord|35|1|24.2|N|138|52|30.8|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 阿波咩命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=長濱神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|1|24.2|N|138|52|30.8|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == 西浦の木負（きしょう）地区の北西、木負堤防が伸びる赤崎の岬の高台に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
江戸時代には「[[神明社]]」と称されていたが、[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
当社は、[[伊豆諸島]]・[[神津島]]の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・[[阿波命神社]]の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、[[事代主命]]（[[三嶋大社|三島大明神]]）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁

== 概要 == [[発端丈山]]の北西山麓、内浦湾の[[三津シーパラダイス]]を見下ろす高台に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

== 概要 == [[発端丈山]]の北西山麓、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある[[三津シーパラダイス]]を見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。

当社は、[[伊豆諸島]]・[[神津島]]の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・[[阿波命神社]]の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、[[事代主命]]（[[三嶋大社|三島大明神]]）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

当社は、[[伊豆諸島]]・[[神津島]]の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・[[阿波命神社]]の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、[[事代主命]]（[[三嶋大社|三島大明神]]）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

revision.comment: 6 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 長濱神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜69 |位置 = {{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 阿波咩命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=長濱神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == [[発端丈山]]の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある[[三津シーパラダイス]]を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
江戸時代には「[[神明社]]」と称されていたが、[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
当社は、[[伊豆諸島]]・[[神津島]]の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・[[阿波命神社]]の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、[[事代主命]]（[[三嶋大社|三島大明神]]）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁
{{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや ぬまつし}} [[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 長濱神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}}} |所在地 = [[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜69 |位置 = {{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 阿波咩命 |社格 = 旧[[村社]]、[[式内社]] |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=長濱神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、[[静岡県]][[沼津市]]内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある[[神社]]<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == [[発端丈山]]の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある[[三津シーパラダイス]]を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の[[式内社]]である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『[[伊豆国神階帳]]』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
江戸時代には「[[神明社]]」と称されていたが、[[明治]]3年（[[1870年]]）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
当社は、[[伊豆諸島]]・[[神津島]]の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・[[阿波命神社]]の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、[[事代主命]]（[[三嶋大社|三島大明神]]）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
明治6年（[[1873年]]）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 外部リンク == * [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁
[[Category:静岡県の旧村社]] [[Category:沼津市の歴史]] [[Category:沼津市の建築物]] [[Category:伊豆国の式内社]]
==End Japanese==

== Japanese Wikipedia content == {{神社 |名称 = 長濱神社 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=沼津市}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}}} |所在地 = {{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜69 |位置 = {{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 阿波咩命 |社格 = 旧{{jalink|村社}}、{{jalink|式内社}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = 10月18日 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shizuoka|label=長濱神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} }} '''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。
== 概要 == {{jalink|発端丈山|Q11579067}}の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある{{jalink|三津シーパラダイス}}を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。
創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。
江戸時代には「{{jalink|神明社}}」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
当社は、{{jalink|伊豆諸島|Q864302}}・{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・{{jalink|阿波命神社|Q17218974}}の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、{{jalink|事代主命}}（{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三島大明神}}）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。
明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。
== 祭神 == * 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）

}}
'''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
{{jalink|発端丈山|Q11579067}}の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある{{jalink|三津シーパラダイス}}を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

江戸時代には「{{jalink|神明社}}」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

当社は、{{jalink|伊豆諸島|Q864302}}・{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・{{jalink|阿波命神社|Q17218974}}の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、{{jalink|事代主命}}（{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三島大明神}}）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
{{jalink|発端丈山|Q11579067}}の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある{{jalink|三津シーパラダイス}}を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

江戸時代には「{{jalink|神明社}}」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

当社は、{{jalink|伊豆諸島|Q864302}}・{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・{{jalink|阿波命神社|Q17218974}}の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、{{jalink|事代主命}}（{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三島大明神}}）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁

'''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
{{jalink|発端丈山|Q11579067}}の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある{{jalink|三津シーパラダイス}}を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

江戸時代には「{{jalink|神明社}}」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

当社は、{{jalink|伊豆諸島|Q864302}}・{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・{{jalink|阿波命神社|Q17218974}}の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、{{jalink|事代主命}}（{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三島大明神}}）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁

== Japanese content ==

|名称   = 長濱神社

{{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''長濱神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ、'''長浜神社'''）は、{{jalink|静岡県|Q131320}}{{jalink|沼津市|Q241037}}内浦長浜（うちうら ながはま）にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁</ref>。

=== 概要 ===
{{jalink|発端丈山|Q11579067}}の北西山麓、長浜地区の北端、内浦湾の沿岸中央にある{{jalink|三津シーパラダイス}}を南側から見下ろす近隣の高台に鎮座している。

創建は不詳だが、同名の{{jalink|式内社}}である'''長浜神社'''（ながはまじんじゃ）に比定され、『{{jalink|伊豆国神階帳|Q17193087}}』にも「従四位上 長瀬の明神」と記されている古社である<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - 玄松子</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - 延喜式神社の調査</ref>。

江戸時代には「{{jalink|神明社}}」と称されていたが、{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}3年（{{jalink|1870年|Q7741}}）に長浜神社へと復名した<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

当社は、{{jalink|伊豆諸島|Q864302}}・{{jalink|神津島|Q301669}}の北西岸・長浜の前に鎮座する式内社・{{jalink|阿波命神社|Q17218974}}の分社であるとする説があり、実際その説に則って、その神社の祭神であり、{{jalink|事代主命}}（{{jalink|三嶋大社|Q650772|三島大明神}}）の正后である阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）が、当社でも祭神になっている<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>。

明治6年（{{jalink|1873年|Q7757}}）に村社に列する<ref name="engi"/>。

=== 祭神 ===
* 阿波咩命（あわのめのみこと）

=== 脚注 ===
{{Reflist}}

=== 外部リンク ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - 静岡県神社庁

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}}}

|位置   = {{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Awanome-no-Mikoto

|神事   =

{{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{jalink|Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067}}, overlooking {{jalink|Mito Sea Paradise}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{jalink|Shinmei-sha}}," but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{jalink|Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} in the {{jalink|Izu Islands|Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{jalink|Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067}}, overlooking {{jalink|Mito Sea Paradise}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{jalink|Shinmei-sha}}," but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{jalink|Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} in the {{jalink|Izu Islands|Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{jalink|Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067}}, overlooking {{jalink|Mito Sea Paradise}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{jalink|Shinmei-sha}}," but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{jalink|Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} in the {{jalink|Izu Islands|Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{jalink|Numazu|Q241037}}, {{jalink|Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{jalink|Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067}}, overlooking {{jalink|Mito Sea Paradise}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{jalink|Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{jalink|Shinmei-sha}}," but in {{jalink|Meiji|Q3125096}} 3 ({{jalink|1870|Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{jalink|Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{jalink|Kōzushima|Q301669}} in the {{jalink|Izu Islands|Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{jalink|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} ({{jalink|Mishima Taisha|Q650772|Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{jalink|1873|Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや ぬまつし}}

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

== Japanese content ==
{{神社
|名称   = Nagahama Shrine
|画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=Numazu City}}
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}}}
|所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}} Uchiura Nagahama 69
|位置   = {{coord|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E|region:JP-22_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
|祭神   = Awanome-no-Mikoto
|社格   = former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}
|創建   =

{{coord2|35|1|7.1|N|138|53|45|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}}
}}
'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067|1=Mount Hottanjō}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===
{{Reflist}}

=== External links ===
* [http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines

{{DEFAULTSORT:なかはましんしや ぬまつし}}

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067|1=Mount Hottanjō}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

=== Notes ===

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|WD=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|WD=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|WD=Q11579067|1=Mount Hottanjō}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|WD=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|WD=Q7741|1=1870}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|WD=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|WD=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|WD=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|WD=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|qid=Q11579067|1=Mount Hottanjō}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|qid=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|qid=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|qid=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)

{{nihongo|'''Nagahama Shrine'''|長濱神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izu Province]].

'''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ, '''長浜神社''') is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Uchiura Nagahama, {{ill|lt=Numazu|Q241037|qid=Q241037|1=Numazu}}, {{ill|lt=Shizuoka Prefecture|Q131320|qid=Q131320|1=Shizuoka Prefecture}}<ref>[http://shizuoka-jinjacho.or.jp/shokai/jinja.php?id=4403279 長浜神社] - Shizuoka Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines</ref>.

=== Overview ===
It stands on nearby high ground at the north end of the Nagahama district, on the northwestern foothills of {{ill|lt=Hottan-jōyama|Q11579067|qid=Q11579067|1=Mount Hottanjō}}, overlooking {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Mito Sea Paradise|1=UNKNOWN}} from the south along the central coast of Uchiura Bay.

Its date of foundation is unknown, but it is identified with the same-named {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, '''Nagahama Shrine''' (ながはまじんじゃ), and is an old shrine also recorded in the ''{{ill|lt=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō|Q17193087|qid=Q17193087|1=Izu no Kuni Shinkai-chō}}'' as "Senior Fourth Rank Upper Grade, Nagase no Myōjin"<ref name="gen">[https://genbu.net/data/izu/nagahama_title.htm 長濱神社] - Genshōshi</ref><ref name="engi">[http://engishiki.org/izu/bun/iz140206-01.html 長浜神社] - Research on Engishiki Shrines</ref>.

In the Edo period it was called "{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}}," but in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1870|Q7741|qid=Q7741|1=1870}}) it restored the name Nagahama Shrine<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

There is a theory that this shrine is a branch of the shikinaisha {{ill|lt=Awanomiya Shrine|Q17218974|qid=Q17218974|1=Awa-no-Mikoto Shrine}}, which stands in front of Nagahama on the northwestern coast of {{ill|lt=Kōzushima|Q301669|qid=Q301669|1=Kōzu-shima}} in the {{ill|lt=Izu Islands|Q864302|qid=Q864302|1=Izu Islands}}. In line with that theory, Awanome-no-Mikoto, the enshrined deity there and the principal consort of {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611}} ({{ill|lt=Mishima Taisha|Q650772|qid=Q650772|lt=Mishima Daimyōjin|1=Mishima Taisha}}), is likewise the enshrined deity here<ref name="gen"/><ref name="engi"/>.

In Meiji 6 ({{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|qid=Q7757|1=1873}}), it was ranked as a village shrine<ref name="engi"/>.

=== Enshrined deity ===
* Awanome-no-Mikoto (あわのめのみこと)
